mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic recovery, driving institutional portfolio rebalancing toward equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day equity returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative following positive INDPRO revisions, or the signal loses predictive power when INDPRO growth exceeds 2 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent indicates maximum monetary accommodation, which eliminates rate hike fears and sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline by more than 3 percent over 20 days in periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and persistently high unemployment reveals labor hoarding and weak job quality, which suppresses wage growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the signal reverses direction when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200,000 monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect renewed consumer confidence and construction sector activity, generating immediate positive price pressure on S&P 500 equities over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST increases, or the signal accuracy drops below 50 percent across multiple housing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production surpasses the 2007 baseline level of 100.0, supply-side constraints ease and corporate margins expand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of periods where INDPRO exceeds 100.0, or the signal loses significance during supply shocks.
